2._mutation_floor_rationale: agent_ids.py is defensive SSM/env I/O error-classification, not parser-like pure logic; a large share of its mutants are equivalent or contrived-only. Owner-set 0.65 (global stays 0.80) — see findings-2-r2.md.

4.mutation_exempt_reason: Phase 4's only pure-logic module is infra/runtime/server.py, a THREADED AgentCore async service-contract shim. mutmut is hostile to it on this host: a mutant that breaks thread termination deadlocks the test subprocess and mutmut 2.5.1's per-mutant timeout does not reap a hung child pytest on Windows (observed: a single run hung 95 min, never scored). This is the same 'wrong instrument for this code' class as phase 2's agent_ids.py, escalated to a hard hang. Phase 4's objective bar is instead: the synth-contract assertions (infra/tests/test_runtime_stack.py - AgentCore Runtime resource shape, no-wildcard IAM, scoped bedrock:InvokeAgent, report-bucket hardening, no NAT) + the offline async-contract test (infra/tests/test_runtime_server.py - non-blocking /invocations, HealthyBusy ping, no-grounding=success, failure surfaced) + changed-line coverage, all run as the panel's regression + coverage legs. Owner decision, 2026-05-17 - see gate run records.
